feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: Who won the Home Run Derby in 2026? Full results, HR totals, highlights from MLB dinger contest
entry.content: Citizens Bank Park saw plenty of blasts on Monday night. The Sporting News tracked live updates, highlights and more as Jordan Walker was victorious at the 2026 Home Run Derby.

entry.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup semifinals will be decided on Colorado grass
entry.content: The 2026 FIFA World Cup semifinals will be decided on grass grown right here in Northern Colorado.

entry.title: Since his brief retirement from Argentina in 2016, Lionel Messi has done nothing but dominate
entry.content: In June 2016, the great Lionel Messi stood in a tunnel underneath MetLife Stadium and announced his retirement from international soccer. We're all blessed that he reconsidered that decision, but no one more so than Argentina's coaches, players and fans.

entry.title: Tony Romo reveals his top career regret in 13 years with the Cowboys
entry.content: Despite playing for 13 years under Jerry Jones and the Dallas Cowboys, Tony Romo still has a big Super Bowl and NFL regret that haunts him.

entry.title: Warriors predicted to sign $73M 17-year veteran, 6-time All-Star as LeBron James decision looms
entry.content: The Golden State Warriors are all-in for LeBron James. However, they could also be signing another veteran wing to be his backup.

entry.title: Lakers predicted to trade Bronny James for freakishly athletic ex-Warriors $46M NBA champion
entry.content: The Los Angeles Lakers are still trying to get a much needed starter at a position of need. In this potential deal they get the starting wing, but have to trade Bronny James in the process.

entry.title: Ex-Laker LeBron James reportedly creates 'league-wide domino effect' as NBA waits on decision
entry.content: LeBron James is nearing a decision. However, there is no movement until James makes his decision as it will completely change the entire free agency market.

entry.title: Most World Cup wins by coach: How France's Didier Deschamps can achieve rare feat in 2026
entry.content: France manager Didier Deschamps could join an exclusive club with a second World Cup win.

entry.title: Pinwheel launches a retro-inspired landline phone for kids
entry.content: Kid-friendly tech company Pinwheel announced the launch of a new landline phone designed to let children stay connected without the distractions of a smartphone.

entry.title: Heat can be deadly, but sunshine itself? Science says we could use more of it | Rowan Jacobsen
entry.content: Extreme exposure should be avoided, but we’ve gone too far the other way – enjoyed safely, the sun can have enormous health benefitsHigh summer has returned to the UK, and with it, the usual warnings about the dangers of sunlight and reminders to seek shade and cover up. After years of such advice, most members of the public naturally assume that the science connecting sun exposure to poor health is well established, so people are often shocked to learn that the opposite is true: those who spend more time in the sun tend to be healthier. A lot healthier.I know because I began researching the subject nine years ago after stumbling upon some studies – and I’ve stayed on the case ever since, now summarising everything we know in my new book, In Defense of Sunlight. It contains good news for many people: we don’t have to fear the sun nearly as much as we thought. In fact, most of us could benefit from a bit more exposure.Rowan Jacobsen is a former Knight Science Journalism Fellow at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) and a media fellow at the Nova Institute for Health in Baltimore. His book In Defense of Sunlight: The Surprising Science of Sun Exposure is published this month Continue reading...

entry.title: 'His legacy will outlive his career': Brendon McCullum drops ultimate praise for Virat Kohli's 300th ODI at Edgebaston
entry.content: Ahead of the India vs England 1st ODI at Edgbaston, coach Brendon McCullum paid a massive tribute to Virat Kohli as the India star prepares for his 300th ODI.

entry.title: Erling Haaland has unexpected impact on newborn babies in Peru after World Cup fame
entry.content: Norway had an amazing run at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, and it’s because of Erling Haaland. The Manchester City superstar bagged seven goals in his first-ever FIFA World Cup and carried the nation on his back.

entry.title: Magic Johnson reveals his biggest Lakers regret, which involves LeBron James
entry.content: Magic Johnson is arguably the greatest Los Angeles Laker of all time, but his biggest regret with the franchise came with his time as an executive in 2019. Johnson spent 13 seasons as a player with the Lakers and has been an ambassador for the franchise his entire adult life.

entry.title: Diego Simeone wants Barcelona wonderkid at Atletico Madrid but club won’t entertain any approach
entry.content: While Barcelona remain focused on finding a formula to sign Julian Alvarez, Atletico Madrid have reportedly identified teenage midfielder Marc Bernal as the player they would like to take in the oppos...

entry.title: Exit-linked Real Madrid midfielder ‘incredibly motivated’ to prove he deserves a place in the team
entry.content: Departures are often difficult at a club like Real Madrid, where players do not really have a reason to leave.Barring a few situations where players may honestly understand their situation and lack of...

entry.title: Real American Freestyle expands with MMA crossover championship division
entry.content: Real American Freestyle has announced a new championship designed exclusively for elite crossover athletes from combat sports.

entry.title: Barcelona prepared to sell Ferran Torres to avoid additional payment to Manchester City
entry.content: Barcelona are prepared to sell Ferran Torres this summer to avoid making an additional payment to Manchester City, per The Athletic. The 26-year-old joined the La Liga club in January 2022 for an ini...

entry.title: When the World Cup finishes, the football continues on BBC Sport
entry.content: When the World Cup ends, regardless of who wins, we'll still have loads of great content every day as we build up to the new Premier League season. As well as all the fallout and reaction to the tournament, we'll quickly shift back towards club football, with pre-season coverage and all the latest transfer gossip and stories. BBC Sport and teams across BBC Nations and Regions cover all 20 Premier League sides and we have dedicated club reporters, who will be travelling to bring you behind-the-scenes insights from pre-season tours.

entry.title: The lonely reality of male infertility – podcast
entry.content: Sperm counts are in decline worldwide, and testosterone levels are said to be plunging, but male infertility still carries a stigma. Prof Michael Carroll on what every man should knowWhen we talk about infertility, the focus tends to be on the woman. After some basic tests, IVF is often seen as a solution. But half of the time, infertility is caused by issues with the man’s sperm. And sperm counts are on the decline worldwide.Toby Trice tells Helen Pidd about how he and his wife spent years trying to conceive and had had two rounds of IVF before they discovered a problem with his testicles was behind their difficulties. He says he struggled with feelings of guilt and shame afterwards. Yet his issues were resolved with a routine operation. Continue reading...

entry.title: 'Listing is a must': Chinese humanoid startups are rushing to launch IPOs
entry.content: Shenzhen-based LimX Dynamics is the latest humanoid company in China to raise capital in recent weeks as investors eye IPO exits.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
Your road to glory will be rocky, but fulfilling.
Lucky Numbers: 16, 98, 89, 96, 51, 93

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Loose power cord? Pinch the prongs together a little to increase its grip.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: On a Canadian two dollar bill, the flag flying over the Parliament buildings is an American flag.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Kamal Ravikant
entry.content: “Pain doesn't last. And when it's gone, we have something to show for it. Growth.” — Kamal Ravikant

entry.title: Michigan health officials identify ‘potential source’ of parasite outbreak
entry.content: Authorities said inquiry into cyclosporiasis outbreak was continuing but early results pointed to salad greens and lettuceWhat is cyclosporiasis, the parasitic illness causing ‘explosive’ diarrhea?State health officials in Michigan said on Monday they have identified lettuces and salad greens as a “potential source” of the thousands of cases of cyclosporiasis – a parasitic infection that causes “watery diarrhea”, loss of appetite and weight loss.In a statement, the Michigan department of health and human services said the investigation is still continuing, but early results “point to lettuce or salad greens as a potential source for this outbreak”, though officials also cautioned it is too early to rule out other food sources. Continue reading...

entry.title: Lettuce or salad greens could be source of diarrhea-causing illness, Michigan health officials say
entry.content: The foodborne gastrointestinal illness has shown up in 31 states, and federal health officials say 86 people have been hospitalized.

entry.title: U.S. strikes Iran for third night after Trump reinstates Iran blockade
entry.content: President Trump ordered a third consecutive night of U.S. strikes in Iran after reinstating a naval blockade of Iranian ports and proposing new fees for ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz. NBC News’ Garrett Haake reports.

entry.title: One of strictest US abortion bans could be overturned in November’s election
entry.content: Idaho to have ballot measure for reproductive freedom law that would reverse ban on abortions at all pregnancy stagesOne of the strictest abortion bans in the country will be on the ballot this November after Idaho’s secretary of state certified a ballot measure on Monday that would reverse the state’s abortion ban that prohibits the procedure at all stages of pregnancy.The ballot initiative was headed by a volunteer-run group called Idahoans United for Women & Families, which ran a petition drive to get the measure in front of voters this fall. They gathered more than 100,000 signatures, surpassing the required 70,725 to get on the ballot. Continue reading...

entry.title: UK children will be one of unhealthiest generations in decades, doctors say
entry.content: Analysis of 12 indicators including asthma, obesity and vaccination finds child health is ‘national embarrassment’Children in the UK will grow up to be one of the unhealthiest generations in decades, with child health outcomes having declined or stalled completely across all areas, a group of leading paediatricians has said.Reduced vaccination rates alongside rising hospital admissions for asthma and mental health disorders are all contributing factors to the UK’s record on children’s health, which should be seen as a “national embarrassment”, their analysis has found. Continue reading...

entry.title: Oil Surges Most Since 2020, Reflecting Bet That Strait Won’t Go Back to Normal
entry.content: The international crude price climbed nearly 10% as President Trump said he would reimpose the U.S. blockade.

entry.title: New details in Mississippi teen’s death
entry.content: As the investigation into the death of 18-year-old Nolan Wells continues, two friends who were on Horn Island are speaking out. Wells went to the island in a boat on July Fourth and his body was found two days later. NBC News’ Kate Snow has the story.

entry.title: In a sweet discovery, astronomers find sugar lurking in the space between stars
entry.content: The space between stars just got a little sweeter.

entry.title: Baseball's All-Stars don't like MLB's salary cap proposal but say there's time to find a deal
entry.content: President Donald Trump on Monday sharply reduced the size of two national monuments in Utah, undoing protections established by former presidents on public lands that are sacred among many Native Americans.

entry.title: Democratic members of U.S. Congress visit Cuba and compare U.S. energy embargo to 'silent Gaza'
entry.content: Four Democratic members of U.S. Congress who traveled to Cuba this weekend described the energy embargo imposed on the island by U.S. President Donald Trump as turning the island into a "silent Gaza."

entry.title: Canadian fashion mogul Peter Nygard pleads guilty in Quebec sex assault case
entry.content: Canadian fashion mogul Peter Nygard pleaded guilty Monday to sexual assault and forcible confinement in Quebec, the latest conviction in the downfall of the founder of the once-global Nygard International clothing company. Nygard also faces U.S. racketeering and sex trafficking charges.

entry.title: Hundreds of economists say 'we must act now' on AI's economic impact and job displacement risks
entry.content: Hundreds of economists say in an open letter that institutions "must act now" to address how artificial intelligence could transform the economy and could put many people out of work.

entry.title: Mexico to file criminal complaints over migrants killed by ICE in US
entry.content: Claudia Sheinbaum says Mexicans ‘outraged’ over killing last week of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo by agents in HoustonMexican president Claudia Sheinbaum announced on Monday that Mexico would be filing criminal complaints in the US for the deaths of more than a dozen Mexican migrants in immigration detention and those killed in anti-migrant operations.The deaths include last week’s killing of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo in Houston, whom Sheinbaum said was “practically murdered”. Continue reading...

entry.title: Federal Immigration Agents Involved in Fatal Shooting in Maine
entry.content: Immigrants rights’ groups said the person killed was a 26-year-old man from Colombia.

entry.title: U.S. announces more strikes on Iran over Strait of Hormuz
entry.content: President Trump said that the United States will blockade Iran in the Strait of Hormuz and charge ships 20% for safe passage. Central Command announced that the U.S. has commenced strikes on Iran for the third straight night. NBC News' Courtney Kube reports.

entry.title: What to Know About Lindsey Graham’s Sister, Darline Graham
entry.content: President Trump backed her to finish her late brother’s term in the Senate.

entry.title: Microsoft is making the Windows Search Box more streamlined and useful
entry.content: Bit by bit, Windows 11 is healing.

entry.title: Satya Nadella has issued a shocking warning to companies using AI
entry.content: Of all the debates raging about the potential downsides of AI, there is one worry causing the most hand-wringing among AI enthusiasts in Silicon Valley — that the giant AI labs that sell proprietary models are somehow acting like Trojan horses.

entry.title: Aaron Rodgers bonds with Steelers teammates on 'last rodeo' out West
entry.content: Aaron Rodgers is having his "last rodeo" literally and figuratively. See how he's bonding with his Steelers teammates ahead of final NFL season.

entry.content: Here's all the info you need in order to watch this year's Home Run Derby, including when it starts and who's competing

entry.title: Rams left tackle Jackson assigned to pre-filing diversion and can avoid domestic violence charge
entry.content: Los Angeles Rams left tackle Alaric Jackson, who was arrested on June 9 on suspicion of felony domestic violence, has the opportunity to avoid criminal charges.

entry.title: 2027 NFL Draft Summer Scouting Report: John Mateer, QB, Oklahoma
entry.content: John Mateer is hoping being healthy this Fall will allow him to remind folks what he is capable of.

entry.title: 2 reasons why Rams' breakout candidate is 231-yard second-round TE
entry.content: This second-round TE for the Rams is headed for a breakout season for 2 reasons.

entry.title: Report: Tom Herman to join Florida State coaching staff ahead of 2026 season
entry.content: On Monday, On3 and Warchant’s Matt LaSerre reported that Tom Herman, a former head coach at Texas, Houston, and FAU, would be joining the Florida State football coaching staff. Herman did not coach in the 2025-26 college football season after being fired from FAU with two games left in the 2024 season. Herman has an […]

entry.title: Panini World Cup stickers: Everything to know about collecting, including where to find and trade in 2026
entry.content: One of the most popular World Cup collectables is the Panini sticker album, which features stickers for many of the 1,248 players across the 48 participating rosters at the 2026 tournament.

entry.title: Michigan hid evidence of multiple scandals, former coach lawsuit says
entry.content: Michigan football allegedly hid evidence of its sign-stealing scandal from 2023 and Sherrone Moore's affair, according to a former coach's lawsuit:

entry.title: Public betas for iOS 27, macOS 27 and more Apple platforms are now available
entry.content: Try the new Siri AI and system-wide performance improvements.

entry.title: Gold’s Faithful Think Twice as Prices Slide From Record Highs
entry.content: After the U.S. and Iran traded a fresh round of attacks, gold took yet another dip.

entry.title: S.C. Gov McMaster appoints Darline Graham Nordone to fill Sen. Lindsey Graham's seat
entry.content: South Carolina Governor Henry McMaster appoints Darline Graham Nordone, Sen. Lindsey Graham's younger sister, to serve the remainder of the late Republican senator's term, which ends early January 2027.

entry.title: I hunted the men who secretly film their wives and share footage online
entry.content: I hunted the men who secretly film their wives and share footage online

entry.title: Josh Grisetti, Broadway actor also known for 'The Marvelous Mrs. Maisel,' dies at 44
entry.content: Josh Grisetti, the Broadway actor who charmed audiences with roles in "Something Rotten" and TV's "The Marvelous Mrs. Maisel," died by suicide. He was 44.

entry.title: Los Angeles law enforcement will stop using Flock cameras
entry.content: The Los Angeles police department did not renew its contract with Flock due to data privacy concerns.

entry.title: Apple says former employee exploited ‘rare’ bug to download confidential files after leaving for OpenAI
entry.content: Apple would not comment on the "security breach," which allegedly allowed a former employee to download sensitive files from Apple's network long after he departed the company for rival OpenAI.

entry.title: Judge Says Trump Tried to Manipulate Legal System With IRS Lawsuit
entry.content: The ruling delivered a scathing rebuke to the president over a case that led to the creation of a $1.8 billion fund and a no-audit pledge.

entry.title: A ransomware negotiator who colluded with attackers to scam victims was sentenced to 70 months in prison
entry.content: Angelo Martino helped scam ransomware victims out of over $75 million, officials said.

entry.title: Michigan cyclosporiasis case count now tops 2,600. Why CDC data is lagging

entry.content: The move would sharpen a standoff over control of Hormuz that has led to days of fighting.

entry.title: Lancashire chemicals factory facing potential legal claim announces closure
entry.content: More than 90 residents have expressed interest in contamination claim against AGC Chemicals EuropeA Pfas factory in Lancashire has announced plans to close down, just days after the Guardian revealed that more than 90 residents had signed up to be involved in a potential legal claim over contamination of the local area.AGC Chemicals Europe is consulting with employees and their union representatives about plans to cease operations at its manufacturing plant in Thornton-Cleveleys, Lancashire. The consultation is expected to last for at least 45 days. Continue reading...

entry.title: America’s Latest Weapon Against Iran: Sea Drones
entry.content: In a U.S. combat first, three unmanned speedboats attacked an Iranian submarine and ship facility.

entry.title: Why the fastest-growing Ebola outbreak in history is becoming more challenging
entry.content: Why the fastest-growing Ebola outbreak in history is becoming more challenging

entry.title: Judge says Trump admin attempted to ‘manipulate the judicial process’ with its IRS settlement
entry.content: A federal judge in Florida has found President Donald Trump’s lawsuit against the Internal Revenue Service was brought in “bad faith” for an “improper purpose.”

entry.title: Oil Tanker Stock Benefits From Strait Of Hormuz Closure
entry.content: Oil Tanker Stock Benefits From Strait Of Hormuz Closure

entry.title: Sam Altman’s space data center trash talk is what most experts already believe
entry.content: Responding to Musk accusing him of being a scammer, Altman said, "homeboy you're the one sellling [sic] public market investors on short-term space datacenters."

entry.title: State AGs sue to block the Paramount-Warner Bros. Discovery merger
entry.content: A dozen state attorneys general filed an antitrust lawsuit to stop the Paramount / Warner Bros. Discovery merger.

entry.content: These overlooked chip stocks are getting an AI boost

entry.title: Investors send General Fusion soaring in debut as first publicly traded fusion company
entry.content: General Fusion started trading on the Nasdaq following a reverse merger that saw high redemptions.

entry.title: Judge blocks Trump settlement with IRS that gave him sweeping tax protections
entry.content: Judge blocks Trump settlement with IRS that gave him sweeping tax protections

entry.title: Counterterrorism Police Probe Death of Former U.K. Government Minister
entry.content: Former Conservative Minister Ann Widdecombe was found dead in her home last week.

entry.title: 12 states sue to block Paramount’s $110B Warner Bros. deal
entry.content: The states allege that the deal would harm movie theaters, basic cable distributors, and audiences.

entry.title: Trump says U.S. will launch an 'Iranian blockade' in the Strait of Hormuz
entry.content: President Trump said the U.S. will reinstate a naval blockade on Iranian ships in the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing attacks between the U.S. and Iran. NBC News' Matt Bradley reports on the ongoing power struggle for control of the strait.

entry.title: Trump recommends Lindsey Graham’s sister to finish his Senate term
entry.content: President Donald Trump said Monday that he has recommended to South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster that he choose Lindsey Graham’s sister, Darline Graham Nordone, to serve the remainder of the late Republican senator’s term, which ends in early January.

entry.title: 12 states file lawsuit to block $110 billion Paramount-Warner Bros. merger
entry.content: Twelve state attorneys general filed a lawsuit challenging Paramount Skydance’s $110 billion acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery. NBC News' Allie Canal breaks down how the lawsuit says consumers could be impacted and what it could mean for Paramount.

entry.title: Should AI help you get away with killing your spouse?
entry.content: What does a world of total user-aligned AI actually look like?

entry.title: Games Done Quick abruptly canceled SNK stream due to ties with Saudi Arabia
entry.content: Games Done Quick said it would not accept funds from or work with SNK in the future.

entry.content: Therefore, here's a look at both the American League and National League rosters for the 2026 All-Star Game.

entry.title: Orioles acquire 8.44 ERA reliever in All-Star break trade with Pirates
entry.content: Baltimore makes an addition to its bullpen.

entry.title: Person reportedly shot and killed in incident involving ICE agent in Maine
entry.content: A person has been reportedly shot and killed in an incident involving an ICE agent in Biddeford, Maine. NBC News' Julia Ainsley has early details on the incident and how local and federal authorities are responding.

entry.title: 'Elite' Azzi Fudd kept Wings' winning streak alive with two clutch plays in 17 seconds
entry.content: The rookie is shining at both ends.

entry.title: Yankees' Ben Rice has a simple plan for MLB Home Run Derby in Philadelphia
entry.content: Ben Rice and his father Dan Rice know what to do during the Derby Monday night.

entry.title: Stretch, be gentle and build flexibility: expert tips on doing the splits
entry.content: Doing a split may look impressive, but experts caution it should not be done without practice and it may not be for everyoneOn Love Island USA’s recent eighth season, contestant Kenzie Annis quickly distinguished herself with her ability to perform the splits, abruptly deploying the maneuver in fits of both delight and rage.Seeing the splits on TV shows such as Love Island and RuPaul’s Drag Race can make people “want to take on that challenge and to push themselves to new heights”, said Ramoni Overton, a yoga instructor and YouTuber based in Los Angeles. Continue reading...

entry.title: Heat named landing spot for $349 million ex-Lakers guard, nine-time All-Star
entry.content: The Miami Heat were linked to a former Los Angeles Lakers backcourt contributor on Sunday.

entry.title: Braves' $20 million infield signing deemed 'massive whiff' at All-Star break
entry.content: This has turned out to be a very bad contract for Atlanta.
